name: Pakistan
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **TRUMP PUBLICLY ENDORSED PAKISTAN MEDIATION + DISCLOSED PAKISTAN ASKED US 'NOT TO MILITARILY ENGAGE IRAN' DURING NEGOTIATIONS — FIRST PUBLIC TRUMP DESCRIPTION OF PAKISTAN AS ACTIVE DIPLOMATIC CONSTRAINT ON US MILITARY OPERATIONS.** Trump May 7-8: talks 'going well,' Tehran wants peace agreement 'a lot more than I do'; Trump praised 'Pakistani civilian and military leaders for their mediation in the conflict' (CONFIRMED — Pakistan Today primary, Arab News PK, Washington Times). Consistent with the pattern of the May 7 destroyer-attack response being limited to port strikes (no Iranian leadership targeting, no IRGC HQ targeting, no nuclear-infrastructure targeting), as Vali Nasr's framework predicted. The public Pakistani-civilian-and-military attribution also reframes — softly — the IRGC's May 7 destroyer attacks as occurring inside a Pakistani-mediated negotiation envelope. **Pakistan's role formalized further:** FM Baghaei Friday continued framing 'Iran is still considering the proposal, the terms of which had not been made public, and would relay their response to Pakistani mediators when it is complete' (CONFIRMED — IRNA, Tasnim via Al Jazeera). However, Pakistani parliamentary spokesperson Rezaei publicly called the US text 'more of an American wishlist than a reality' — Pakistani-side dissent visible inside the channel, possibly reflecting Iran-aligned parliamentary faction views distinct from Sharif-Munir executive line. Saudi public denial of WSJ scoop (via Al-Hadath) explicitly endorsed 'Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement' — Pakistan now has Saudi public backing on the mediation track. Architecture: Pakistan PRIMARY, supported by direct Witkoff/Kushner channel + Saudi soft-support; Russia uranium-custodian track sidelined. Munir-Sharif channel verified-functional through May 7-8 stress. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Pakistan's mediation produced (a) May 5 pause framework, (b) 14-point MOU drafting, (c) explicit 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision, (d) Iran's first unconditional Hormuz reopening offer. If Iran formally signs by May 14 (Trump 1-week clock) or May 21 (Sadjadpour falsification), Pakistan emerges as structural winner of the diplomatic architecture.

active_triggers.condition: Pakistan hosts direct US-Iran face-to-face talks
Pakistan-brokered Iranian unified proposal delivered to US within 21 days (Munir-Sharif channel)

Trump May 7-8: talks 'going well,' Tehran wants peace agreement 'a lot more than I do'; praised 'Pakistani civilian and military leaders for their mediation in the conflict.' recent_signals.headline: PAKISTAN MEDIATION DELIVERED 14-POINT MOU TEXT — Witkoff and Kushner negotiating directly with Iranian officials AND through Pakistani mediators. Pakistan PM Shehbaz: 'grateful to Trump for courageous leadership.' Russia uranium-custodian role potentially REACTIVATED for HEU custody under MOU (Peskov reaffirmed offer 'remains active' but US has rejected; alternatives IAEA, Kazakhstan). Architecture: Pakistan PRIMARY, supported by direct Witkoff/Kushner channel.
